message:
refactor(math): replace std intrinsics with libm for cross-platform determinism

Switch all math function implementations in frankenlibc-core from Rust
std method calls (e.g. x.exp(), x.sin(), x.abs()) to explicit libm
crate calls (e.g. libm::exp(x), libm::sin(x), libm::fabs(x)).

This ensures bit-exact reproducible results across compilers and
platforms, which is critical for the determinism proof verification
gate. The libm crate provides software implementations that do not
depend on platform-specific hardware FP instructions.

Changes:
- Cargo.toml: add libm = "0.2" dependency
- exp.rs: exp, log, log10, pow -> libm equivalents
- float.rs: fabs, ceil, floor, round, fmod -> libm equivalents
- trig.rs: sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2 -> libm equivalents
- special.rs: replace hand-rolled Abramowitz-Stegun erf and Lanczos
  tgamma/lgamma approximations with libm::erf/tgamma/lgamma

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
